title: Build a Light Sensor
description: Use a photoresistor to measure ambient brightness with an Arduino.

dialogue.text: Hey again! Let's hook up a photoresistor so the Arduino can detect light levels.
Place the photoresistor and a 10 kΩ resistor on the breadboard to form a voltage divider. Connect the junction to A0 on the Arduino and power the circuit.
Upload a sketch that reads the analog value from A0 and prints it to the serial monitor. Watch the numbers change as light levels vary.
Nice work! You've built a basic light sensor for your future projects.
